briefs.title: CapyChill 每日專輯｜海邊黃昏・Slow Notes by the Bay
briefs.focus: 10 首 × 約 3 分鐘＝約 30–35 分鐘
briefs.meta: 固定海邊書桌母場景 · 10 音樂＋1 概念圖＋6 段微動畫
briefs.summary: 本批目標 30 分鐘，安排 6 段不同微動作。先只生成第 1 條基準驗收片；確認海浪、倒影、角色結構與循環正常後，才生成其餘項目。規則：30 分鐘＝6 段；45 分鐘＝8 段；60 分鐘＝10 段。

items.purpose: 30 分鐘 YouTube Lo-fi 長片
items.text: Track 1/10 — "Opening the Window". Create a 3:00–3:30 instrumental lo-fi track for one cohesive 30-minute CapyChill album titled "海邊黃昏・Slow Notes by the Bay". Mood: warm, unhurried, quietly optimistic. 70 BPM, 4/4, Rhodes piano and felt piano, warm restrained bass and soft brushed or tape-muted drums. Composition: simple four-chord welcome; introduce a small variation after 60 seconds, a calm B section near 1:30, then return to the main motif. Mix for long listening: no vocals, no spoken words, no dramatic drops, no piercing highs, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation. Leave gentle headroom and end cleanly with a 4–6 second natural tail. Environmental texture: soft sea breeze and distant water ambience, very low in the mix. This track must feel related to the other nine tracks without reusing the same lead melody.

items.purpose: 固定場景 reference
items.text: Create one high-resolution 16:9 hand-painted storybook master frame for CapyChill, ideally 3840×2160. Preserve only the channel identity: the same warm-brown capybara design, cream headphones, relaxed eyes, natural anatomy and hand-painted storybook rendering. Do not reuse another day's room, furniture layout or character position. TODAY'S ORIGINAL SETTING: an open seaside writing alcove with a wide bay window and a warm walnut desk. Place the capybara at warm walnut writing desk with the centre of its head near 64% of image width. Camera is locked, eye-level and calm, using a 35mm-equivalent wide view appropriate to this setting. Today’s album is 海邊黃昏・Slow Notes by the Bay; use peach sunset, muted ocean blue, warm walnut. TODAY'S STORY: a quiet sunset journaling session before evening. The capybara is writing a short gratitude note in an open cream notebook. Include these scene-specific props: a dark ceramic coffee mug, one shell paperweight, a folded postcard and a slim sandalwood incense holder placed away from the character silhouette. Include a tiny ginger cat curled in a light oatmeal cushion below the desk. Make the following physical motion opportunities clearly visible and spatially separated: several small ocean wave bands travel toward shore, fine ripples change continuously, and the golden reflection shimmers softly without reversing; one narrow coffee-steam ribbon rises and dissipates in clear air without crossing the character; the curtain edge and two plant leaves sway once in the same gentle sea breeze. Calm original illustration, clean natural capybara anatomy. No text, letters, numbers, logos, watermark, signature, sparkle icon or fake signage. Output one clean reference image, not a collage. Each prop must have a clear resting position and must never compete with the capybara. COMPOSE FOR DUAL FORMAT: the full 16:9 frame must work as a YouTube video, and a narrow 9:16 portrait crop around the character must also work as a complete Shorts/Reels frame. The intended portrait crop spans approximately 44% to 76% of the original width, centred near 64%. Do not draw crop guides. Inside that corridor keep the entire head, headphones, primary paw action, main working surface, companion, one key prop and enough of today's environment to tell where the scene takes place. Keep smoke, steam and moving objects in independent clear air paths that never cross the character silhouette or other solid objects. Leave breathing room above the head and below the main action for vertical-platform UI overlays. This image will become an image-to-video reference, so use clean separable visual layers for the character, companion, foreground props, moving environmental elements and background.

items.engine: Veo 3.1 Lite 測試 → Fast 定稿
items.text: 8-second 16:9 image-to-video from the selected CapyChill master frame. 基準驗收・場景動態. STATIC STRUCTURE: locked camera; architecture, furniture, solid props and room geometry do not move. Preserve the character's identity, proportions and silhouette. No cut, zoom, pan or artificial parallax. MANDATORY BASE ENVIRONMENT MOTION THROUGHOUT THE ENTIRE CLIP: several small ocean wave bands travel toward shore, fine ripples change continuously, and the golden reflection shimmers softly without reversing. Motion remains slow, subtle and physically consistent—never frozen, reversed, pulsing, teleporting or moving as one flat sheet. CHARACTER/PROP ACTION FOR THIS CLIP ONLY: The capybara holds the original pose. Only slow breathing and one soft blink. Prioritize the scene's mandatory environmental motion, stable anatomy and a clean loop. All other optional props and the companion stay still unless this clip explicitly names them. Preserve the exact weather shown in the reference; do not invent additional rain, snow, smoke, fire, wind or lighting effects. End close to the opening pose and environmental motion phase for a gentle loop; a short crossfade will be added in editing, so do not force an abrupt rewind. No new objects, morphing, extra limbs, floating pencil, liquid volume change, smoke through objects, moving furniture, sudden light pulse, text, logo or dialogue. Keep the action and primary moving prop inside the planned 9:16 crop corridor.

items.purpose: 30 分鐘 YouTube Lo-fi 長片
items.text: Track 1/10 — "Opening the Window". Create a 3:00–3:30 instrumental lo-fi track for one cohesive 30-minute CapyChill album titled "深夜讀寫・Midnight Pages". Mood: deep focus, calm solitude, never gloomy. 70 BPM, 4/4, Rhodes piano and felt piano, warm restrained bass and soft brushed or tape-muted drums. Composition: simple four-chord welcome; introduce a small variation after 60 seconds, a calm B section near 1:30, then return to the main motif. Mix for long listening: no vocals, no spoken words, no dramatic drops, no piercing highs, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation. Leave gentle headroom and end cleanly with a 4–6 second natural tail. Environmental texture: subtle night room tone and faint sea wash, very low in the mix. This track must feel related to the other nine tracks without reusing the same lead melody.

items.purpose: 固定場景 reference
items.text: Create one high-resolution 16:9 hand-painted storybook master frame for CapyChill, ideally 3840×2160. Preserve only the channel identity: the same warm-brown capybara design, cream headphones, relaxed eyes, natural anatomy and hand-painted storybook rendering. Do not reuse another day's room, furniture layout or character position. TODAY'S ORIGINAL SETTING: a compact midnight library loft with tall bookshelves, a sloped window and an amber reading lamp. Place the capybara at a low dark-oak library table with the centre of its head near 66% of image width. Camera is locked, eye-level and calm, using a 35mm-equivalent wide view appropriate to this setting. Today’s album is 深夜讀寫・Midnight Pages; use navy night, warm amber pool of light, soft moon silver. TODAY'S STORY: a calm midnight reading and annotation session. The capybara is reading an open book with one paw resting beside a fountain pen. Include these scene-specific props: a navy tea mug, two neatly stacked books, a brass bookmark and a narrow hinoki incense holder in a clear side area. Include a tiny dormouse asleep under a small indigo blanket in a shelf nook. Make the following physical motion opportunities clearly visible and spatially separated: moonlit clouds drift extremely slowly beyond the sloped window and a few dust motes cross the lamp beam; one page corner lifts slightly and settles without turning the page; a thin hinoki smoke trail rises within its own clear air column and never overlaps the character. Calm original illustration, clean natural capybara anatomy. No text, letters, numbers, logos, watermark, signature, sparkle icon or fake signage. Output one clean reference image, not a collage. Each prop must have a clear resting position and must never compete with the capybara. COMPOSE FOR DUAL FORMAT: the full 16:9 frame must work as a YouTube video, and a narrow 9:16 portrait crop around the character must also work as a complete Shorts/Reels frame. The intended portrait crop spans approximately 48% to 80% of the original width, centred near 66%. Do not draw crop guides. Inside that corridor keep the entire head, headphones, primary paw action, main working surface, companion, one key prop and enough of today's environment to tell where the scene takes place. Keep smoke, steam and moving objects in independent clear air paths that never cross the character silhouette or other solid objects. Leave breathing room above the head and below the main action for vertical-platform UI overlays. This image will become an image-to-video reference, so use clean separable visual layers for the character, companion, foreground props, moving environmental elements and background.

Place the capybara at a round weathered picnic table with the centre of its head near 46% of image width. Camera is locked, eye-level and calm, using a 35mm-equivalent wide view appropriate to this setting. Today’s album is 週末慢拍・A Very Slow Sunday; use soft terracotta, cream, sun-washed blue. TODAY'S STORY: an easy Sunday sketching break. The capybara is making a tiny plant sketch in the notebook. Include these scene-specific props: a cream cocoa mug, one small pastry plate, a blank postcard and three coloured pencils held safely in a cup. Include a tiny yellow duckling asleep in a shallow woven basket beside a planter. Keep smoke, steam and moving objects in independent clear air paths that never cross the character silhouette or other solid objects. Leave breathing room above the head and below the main action for vertical-platform UI overlays. This image will become an image-to-video reference, so use clean separable visual layers for the character, companion, foreground props, moving environmental elements and background.

briefs.summary: 本批目標 30 分鐘，安排 6 段不同微動作。先只生成第 1 條基準驗收片；確認海浪、倒影、角色結構與循環正常後，才生成其餘項目。規則：30 分鐘＝6 段；45 分鐘＝8 段；60 分鐘＝10 段。

items.purpose: 30 分鐘 YouTube Lo-fi 長片
items.text: Track 1/10 — "Opening the Window". Create a 3:00–3:30 instrumental lo-fi track for one cohesive 30-minute CapyChill album titled "月光晚安・Moonlit Capy". Mood: sleepy, safe, tender and low-energy. 70 BPM, 4/4, Rhodes piano and felt piano, warm restrained bass and soft brushed or tape-muted drums. Composition: simple four-chord welcome; introduce a small variation after 60 seconds, a calm B section near 1:30, then return to the main motif. Mix for long listening: no vocals, no spoken words, no dramatic drops, no piercing highs, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation. Leave gentle headroom and end cleanly with a 4–6 second natural tail. Environmental texture: slow waves and extremely soft night ambience, very low in the mix. This track must feel related to the other nine tracks without reusing the same lead melody.

items.purpose: 固定場景 reference
items.text: Create one high-resolution 16:9 hand-painted storybook master frame for CapyChill, ideally 3840×2160. Preserve only the channel identity: the same warm-brown capybara design, cream headphones, relaxed eyes, natural anatomy and hand-painted storybook rendering. Do not reuse another day's room, furniture layout or character position. TODAY'S ORIGINAL SETTING: a cosy open-front canvas tent beside a moonlit lake, with pine silhouettes and a low lantern. Place the capybara at a low folding camp table with the centre of its head near 55% of image width. Camera is locked, eye-level and calm, using a 35mm-equivalent wide view appropriate to this setting. Today’s album is 月光晚安・Moonlit Capy; use indigo, moon cream, very low amber light. TODAY'S STORY: a gentle final note before sleep. The capybara is writing one last short line before closing the notebook. Include these scene-specific props: a moon-cream warm milk mug, a closed storybook, a soft cloth bookmark and a safe covered lantern. Include a tiny brown hedgehog asleep under a moon-cream miniature blanket in a padded basket. Make the following physical motion opportunities clearly visible and spatially separated: small lake ripples move outward naturally, pine tips sway slightly, and three distant fireflies drift independently without blinking in sync; warm milk steam rises in one faint ribbon and dissipates before reaching the character; the tent flap moves once and the hedgehog blanket rises subtly with one slow breath. Calm original illustration, clean natural capybara anatomy. No text, letters, numbers, logos, watermark, signature, sparkle icon or fake signage. Output one clean reference image, not a collage. Each prop must have a clear resting position and must never compete with the capybara. COMPOSE FOR DUAL FORMAT: the full 16:9 frame must work as a YouTube video, and a narrow 9:16 portrait crop around the character must also work as a complete Shorts/Reels frame. The intended portrait crop spans approximately 37% to 69% of the original width, centred near 55%. Do not draw crop guides. Inside that corridor keep the entire head, headphones, primary paw action, main working surface, companion, one key prop and enough of today's environment to tell where the scene takes place. Keep smoke, steam and moving objects in independent clear air paths that never cross the character silhouette or other solid objects. Leave breathing room above the head and below the main action for vertical-platform UI overlays. This image will become an image-to-video reference, so use clean separable visual layers for the character, companion, foreground props, moving environmental elements and background.

Mood: cool, restorative, quietly creative. 70 BPM, 4/4, Rhodes piano and felt piano, warm restrained bass and soft brushed or tape-muted drums. Composition: simple four-chord welcome; introduce a small variation after 60 seconds, a calm B section near 1:30, then return to the main motif. Mix for long listening: no vocals, no spoken words, no dramatic drops, no piercing highs, no recognizable melody, no artist imitation. Leave gentle headroom and end cleanly with a 4–6 second natural tail. Environmental texture: a shallow stream and soft bamboo leaves, very low in the mix. This track must feel related to the other nine tracks without reusing the same lead melody.

items.purpose: 固定場景 reference
items.text: Create one high-resolution 16:9 hand-painted storybook master frame for CapyChill, ideally 3840×2160. Preserve only the channel identity: the same warm-brown capybara design, cream headphones, relaxed eyes, natural anatomy and hand-painted storybook rendering. Do not reuse another day's room, furniture layout or character position. TODAY'S ORIGINAL SETTING: a shaded open-air bamboo tea pavilion beside a shallow forest stream. Place the capybara at a low reclaimed-wood tea table with the centre of its head near 38% of image width. Camera is locked, eye-level and calm, using a 35mm-equivalent wide view appropriate to this setting. Today’s album is 竹影午後・Notes by the Stream; use bamboo green, river-stone grey, warm tea brown. TODAY'S STORY: a cool afternoon watercolor break beside moving water. The capybara is adding one pale green wash to a small watercolor postcard. Include these scene-specific props: a celadon tea cup, a flat watercolor palette, one bamboo brush rest and a small river stone. Include a tiny red panda asleep on a woven cushion beside the table. Make the following physical motion opportunities clearly visible and spatially separated: shallow stream ripples travel steadily downstream around fixed stones, while separate bamboo leaf clusters sway gently in one consistent breeze; one narrow tea-steam ribbon rises and disperses before reaching the character; a small bamboo wind bell swings once through a short natural arc while its hanging paper remains intact. Calm original illustration, clean natural capybara anatomy. No text, letters, numbers, logos, watermark, signature, sparkle icon or fake signage. Output one clean reference image, not a collage. Each prop must have a clear resting position and must never compete with the capybara. COMPOSE FOR DUAL FORMAT: the full 16:9 frame must work as a YouTube video, and a narrow 9:16 portrait crop around the character must also work as a complete Shorts/Reels frame. The intended portrait crop spans approximately 20% to 52% of the original width, centred near 38%. Do not draw crop guides. Inside that corridor keep the entire head, headphones, primary paw action, main working surface, companion, one key prop and enough of today's environment to tell where the scene takes place. Keep smoke, steam and moving objects in independent clear air paths that never cross the character silhouette or other solid objects. Leave breathing room above the head and below the main action for vertical-platform UI overlays. This image will become an image-to-video reference, so use clean separable visual layers for the character, companion, foreground props, moving environmental elements and background.
